intro: Bulbostylis equitans, also known as corkscrew sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbostylis equitans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make cough syrups and expectorant teas to relieve respiratory congestion.
